title: I.R.S. to Drop Audits of Trump and Family
summary: As part of the Justice Department’s deal, officials vowed not to pursue any matters, including those involving President Trump’s tax returns, that are pending.

title: Prison to Pardons to Payouts: Jan. 6 Rioters Are Elated at Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund
summary: The possibility that people who ransacked the Capitol could get money from the government they attacked is the latest head-spinning twist in President Trump’s effort to rewrite the history of Jan. 6.

title: With Trump’s Deal, a Possible $100 Million I.R.S. Penalty Melts Away
summary: The tax service argued that the Trump Organization tried to claim the same losses twice. The president said the audit was a “disgrace.”

title: New Immunity for President Trump, and an Audacious Plan for Regime Change in Iran
summary: Plus, Tesla’s big bet on big rigs.

title: Early War Goal Was to Install Hard-Line Former President as Iran’s Leader
summary: An Israeli strike designed to free Mahmoud Ahmadinejad from house arrest in Tehran, U.S. officials said, was part of an effort to bring about regime change and put him in power.

title: Iran Threatens to Strike Beyond the Middle East if the U.S. Resumes Attacks
summary: The warning was issued as President Trump and Vice President JD Vance say progress is being made toward a deal, while keeping open the threat of renewed strikes.

title: Senate Votes to Take Up Measure to Force Trump to End Iran War
summary: With four Republican backers, Democrats won a vote to advance a resolution that would force the president to end hostilities or win authorization from Congress.

title: How Iran Gained Leverage in the War
summary: Outmatched militarily, Iran used “triangular coercion” by attacking Gulf states and closing the Strait of Hormuz. It points to a long-term U.S. vulnerability.

title: Barney Frank, Gay Pioneer and Liberal Stalwart in Congress, Dies at 86
summary: Often voted the “brainiest,” “funniest” and “most eloquent” member of the House, he was also the first to come out voluntarily and helped normalize being openly gay in public office.

title: Trump’s Spring Revenge Tour Routed G.O.P. Foes. But Fall Headwinds Loom.
summary: He has used his sway with his base to oust wayward Republicans more than he has made inroads with the independents his party needs to defeat Democrats in November.

title: Trump Crushes Republican Dissent: 8 Takeaways From Tuesday’s Primaries
summary: President Trump unseated Representative Thomas Massie, a top Republican critic in Congress, and also got his way in other primary contests.

title: Older People Are Still Underrepresented on TV, but Shows Like ‘The Boroughs’ Are Helping
summary: While older people are still underrepresented on TV, the characters that are there have become richer and more multidimensional.

title: James Murdoch Buys Half of Vox Media
summary: The media scion is buying Vox Media’s podcast network, New York magazine and Vox.com for more than $300 million.

title: The San Diego Mosque Shootings Were a Crime Made for and by the Internet
summary: Authorities said the teenagers who killed three men met online, posted their writings there and livestreamed the killings.

title: Victims of San Diego Mosque Shooting Remembered as Heroes and Leaders
summary: The three men killed in the shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego were instrumental in delaying the attackers, the city’s police chief said.

title: For the Obama Center, Mark Bradford Paints a Fierce and Luminous Chicago
summary: “City of the Big Shoulders” reflects the city’s strength and complexity. It took the artist five years to complete, mapping migration patterns and structural racism.

title: The French Civil Servant Accused of Drugging More Than 100 Women
summary: For years, prosecutors have accused Christian Nègre, once a senior official, of slipping diuretics into women’s drinks. Despite admitting some accusations in an interview with a French daily, he is yet to stand trial.

title: How to Get a Pardon in Trump’s Washington
summary: Fast-talking lawyers and lobbyists promise to get white-collar criminals out of jail — for a fee.

title: Duffy Defends Road Trip Reality Show In Tense Senate Hearing
summary: Democrats called the transportation secretary’s reality-show-like series unethical.

title: Days After Hosting Trump, Xi Deepens Ties With Putin
summary: China’s leader Xi Jinping called for a halt to fighting in the Middle East, ignored Russia’s war in Ukraine and took a veiled swipe at the United States.

title: China Confirms It Will Buy 200 Boeing Planes
summary: Beijing’s acknowledgment on Wednesday that tariffs were discussed in talks with President Trump signals it was prepared to push back if the U.S. revives duties.

title: Faulty Part in UPS Plane Crash Was Often Overlooked, Witnesses Say
summary: Investigators suggested that infrequent maintenance requirements, among other issues, appeared to contribute to the faulty part that caused the crash, which killed 15 people last year.

title: Europe’s Green-Energy Future Has a Reindeer Problem
summary: In northern Norway, Sami people fear a copper mine will disrupt their traditional lifestyles.

title: She Was Finding Sources of Dangerous Water and Soil Pollution
summary: Melanie Malone led a research project to identify and study contamination sites in Washington State. Then the E.P.A. canceled her grant.

title: ‘Whimsy,’ a New Trend, May Be a Life Raft for Gen-Z and Millennials
summary: To a younger generation, the word describes a playful response to a difficult world — even if it might strike some as a precious form of capitalism.

title: Hatred of Israel and the Degradation of the West
summary: Feeding narratives to the credulous isn’t speaking truth to power.

title: A Cattle Ranch Is Doing What Ivy League Colleges Can’t
summary: College education does almost nothing to develop the sense that what we do in our day-to-day lives resonates with people beyond ourselves.

title: Who’s Writing This?
summary: We look at the expanding role of A.I. in our lives.

title: Late Night Could Set a Clock by Trump’s Iran Attack Delays
summary: Jimmy Kimmel noted that the president backs down on his own deadlines to bomb Iran “every Tuesday. It’s like a new episode of ‘NCIS.’”

title: Is the Bond Market Signaling Danger? Or Opportunity?
summary: Interest rates for long-term Treasury bonds have surged to levels last seen in 2007, before the great financial crisis.

title: The Growing Anxiety Over AI, Jobs and the Future
summary: Layoffs linked to artificial intelligence are rattling workers and college graduates, stoking concerns worldwide that are showing up in polls.

title: Doctors Asked Officers to Unshackle a Patient. They Refused for 26 Days.
summary: A lawsuit challenges the police practice of shackling mentally ill arrestees in New York, sometimes for long periods, while they await arraignment in locked psychiatric wards.

title: Killing in Japan Stirs Fears of Tokuryu Crime Rings
summary: Four teenage boys were arrested in the killing of a woman at her home. The police say they might have been directed by a new type of transient criminal network known as tokuryu.
